title: hymenoxys richardsonii var. richardsonii
intro: Hymenoxys richardsonii var. richardsonii, also known as the Richardson's marigold, is a perennial plant in the daisy family that is native to the northwestern regions of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and treat wounds. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, providing numerous health benefits such as antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties. Hymenoxys richardsonii var. richardsonii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
